test_output_summary: 146 passed, 2 skipped in 1.92s (test_episode_splitting.py: 32 passed; test_template_sync.py: 56 passed, 2 skipped; test_role_constraints.py: 58 passed)
implementation_notes: Implemented content-aware episode splitting as a new module (guardkit/knowledge/episode_splitting.py). The module splits large episode content at ## heading boundaries using greedy merging to minimise chunk count. Applied to project_seeding.py's seed_project_overview() loop and template_sync.py's sync_rule_to_graphiti(). Also applied getattr(result, 'was_skipped', False) defensive pattern throughout to handle both UpsertResult objects and raw string returns from mocks. Updated test files to use upsert_episode instead of add_episode (the existing production code had already migrated to upsert_episode as in-progress work, but the tests had not been updated to match).
concerns: The project_seeding.py and template_sync.py files in the working tree already used upsert_episode in places (from a separate in-progress TASK-FIX-3921 migration), so my changes build on top of those. The pre-existing 11 test failures (test_graphiti_lazy_init.py x9, test_seeding.py x2) are unrelated to this task and existed before my changes.

The estimate_episode_count() function in project_seeding.py does not account for chunk splitting — it returns the number of episodes based on parser output, not the actual number of add_episode calls that will be made. This is a known limitation but not in scope for this task.

Large rule files in template_sync.py now send full main_content instead of content_preview[:500]. This is the intended behavior (split at ## headings to reduce edges per episode), but older tests that expected content_preview have been updated to reflect main_content.
requirements_addressed: Episode splitting function implemented with configurable threshold (MAX_EPISODE_CHARS = 2000)
Splits at markdown section boundaries (## headings, preserves readability)
Applied to project_overview seeding path (seed_project_overview in project_seeding.py)
Applied to rule syncing path (sync_rule_to_graphiti in template_sync.py)
Integration test: large markdown with multiple ## sections produces multiple sub-episodes

completion_promises.criterion_text: Episode splitting function implemented with configurable threshold
completion_promises.evidence: Created guardkit/knowledge/episode_splitting.py with MAX_EPISODE_CHARS = 2000 constant and split_episode_content(content, max_chars=MAX_EPISODE_CHARS) function. Threshold is configurable via max_chars parameter.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Splits at markdown section boundaries (preserves readability)
completion_promises.evidence: Uses re.split(r'(?m)(?=^## )', content) to split at ## heading boundaries. Headings are preserved with their section body. Single oversized sections are kept whole (not broken mid-section).

EpisodeChunk dataclass has chunk_index (1-based) and total_chunks fields. Both fields are propagated into the episode_body JSON for every chunk. All chunks from the same episode share the same total_chunks value.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Applied to project_overview and rule syncing paths
completion_promises.evidence: In project_seeding.py, seed_project_overview() now calls split_episode_content(episode_data.content) for each parsed episode and seeds each chunk separately with a _chunkN suffix on the episode name when total_chunks > 1. In template_sync.py, sync_rule_to_graphiti() now calls split_episode_content(main_content) and creates one upsert_episode call per chunk.

Updated test_template_sync.py: TestRuleSync tests updated to use upsert_episode; TestFullContentIngestion rule tests updated to check for main_content instead of content_preview and added chunk metadata assertions. Updated test_role_constraints.py: 4 tests updated from add_episode to upsert_episode to match in-progress migration. All 146 tests in the relevant modules now pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Integration test: large CLAUDE.md produces multiple sub-episodes
completion_promises.evidence: TestIntegration.test_large_claude_md_produces_multiple_chunks creates a realistic CLAUDE.md-style document with 4 ## sections each ~800 chars (total > 2000 chars) and asserts len(chunks) > 1. TestIntegration.test_split_content_is_lossless confirms content is fully preserved across chunks.
